metadata.seed: Well, I feel really confident in that moment. It feels very real. Like any moment they're going to kick me out. I, like, I'm just not doing well.

patient.query: Well, I feel really confident in that moment. It feels very real. Like any moment they're going to kick me out. I, like, I'm just not doing well.

automatic_thoughts: they're going to kick me out
I'm just not doing well

llm_response.response:
FINAL RESPONSE:
"It sounds like you're experiencing a mix of confidence and fear right now. The worry about being kicked out seems to be creating a lot of tension for you, even though you feel capable in the moment. Can you tell me more about what makes you feel both confident and uncertain at the same time?"

patient.query: Yeah, I guess it does feel like a tug-of-war sometimes. Like, when I'm doing something I know I can do well, there’s this spark of confidence, you know? But then, out of nowhere, that fear creeps in, and I start thinking, “What if I mess it up?” It’s like I always have this little voice saying I’m not good enough, like I don’t really belong.

triggers: doing something I know I can do well
automatic_thoughts: What if I mess it up?
I'm not good enough
I don't really belong

entailment: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing some internal conflict between confidence and fear, especially when you're doing something familiar. That little voice you mentioned seems to be creating a lot of tension for you. Can you tell me more about how that feeling of not belonging shows up in your daily life?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s weird. I mean, I can be at a party or even just hanging out with friends, and I’ll start to feel really out of place. Like everyone else is having fun and connecting, and I’m just standing there, feeling like I’m not doing it right or something. It makes me want to just retreat or stay quiet, even when I want to join in. It’s frustrating because I know I can be fun, but then that feeling gets in the way.

automatic_thoughts: I’m not doing it right
everyone else is having fun and connecting

neutral: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're experiencing moments where social interactions feel challenging, and you might be noticing some patterns in how you respond. Can you tell me more about times when you felt more connected or at ease in similar situations?

patient.query: Hmm, there have definitely been times when I felt more relaxed, like when I’m with my close friends. We joke around, and I can be myself without second-guessing every word. It’s just easy, you know? But even then, I sometimes worry about saying something dumb and ruining the vibe. It’s like I’m constantly balancing between feeling comfortable and then spiraling into self-doubt.

automatic_thoughts: worry about saying something dumb
ruining the vibe
balancing between feeling comfortable and self-doubt

entailment: Fear of saying the wrong thing (finding)
neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like you're noticing a tension between moments of ease in social settings and your internal worries about how you're coming across. Can you tell me more about what feels different when you're relaxed versus when you're caught up in those self-doubting thoughts?

patient.query: Yeah, when I’m relaxed, it’s like the pressure just lifts. I can laugh, and I don’t think about every little thing I say or do. I feel lighter, and I can just enjoy the moment. But when those self-doubts hit, everything feels heavy. I start overthinking every joke or story, and it’s like my brain goes into overdrive, making me feel small and awkward. It’s exhausting to switch between those feelings. It’s like I’m two

automatic_thoughts: I overthink every joke or story
my brain goes into overdrive
I feel small and awkward

neutral: Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

llm_response.response: That's an interesting way to describe it - like there's a shift between feeling light and heavy. When you say your brain goes into overdrive, what makes you feel like you're stuck in that mode?

patient.query: I guess it’s when I start comparing myself to others. Like, I’ll see someone being really charismatic or confident, and then I just feel so... ordinary. It’s like a switch flips, and I can’t help but think about all the things I’m not doing right. Then I start getting lost in my thoughts, and it’s hard to pull myself back to just enjoying things. It’s almost like I get trapped in this loop, and all the fun fades away.

automatic_thoughts: I’m not doing things right
I feel ordinary

llm_response.response: It sounds like seeing others' strengths can really trigger these thoughts about yourself. Can you tell me more about how those thoughts feel when they start?

patient.query: Yeah, it’s like... they just flood in, you know? At first, it’s just this tiny whisper of insecurity, but then it grows into this loud voice that tells me I'm not enough. I can feel my chest tightening, and suddenly I’m not just seeing their strengths; I’m feeling all my weaknesses highlighted. It’s a mix of envy and disappointment in myself, like I’m letting something slip away that I really want. It’s just... heavy.

automatic_thoughts: I'm not enough
I'm letting something slip away

neutral: Tends to turn anger against self (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those feelings of insecurity can really build and intensify, making you feel overwhelmed by self-doubt and highlighting your shortcomings. The way envy and disappointment mix with that heaviness must be incredibly difficult to carry.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is. It’s like I’m carrying around this backpack full of all those doubts and worries. Sometimes I even feel like I'm trapped in my own head, just wishing I could break free and see things differently. It’s tiring, and I want to shake off that weight, but it feels like it’s become part of me somehow. I just wish I could feel more at ease with myself, like I’m allowed to take up space without all that baggage.

automatic_thoughts: I'm carrying around doubts and worries
I'm trapped in my own head
I wish I could break free and see things differently
It feels like it's become part of me
I wish I could feel more at ease with myself
I'm not allowed to take up space
behaviors: want to shake off that weight

llm_response.response: It sounds like those doubts and worries have become a heavy burden for you, making it hard to feel at ease with yourself. The feeling of being trapped in your own head and wishing you could break free seems to be weighing on you.

patient.query: It really is heavy. I feel like I’m carrying around this backpack full of all those doubts and worries. Sometimes I feel trapped in my own head, wishing I could break free and see things differently. It's so tiring, and I want to shake off that weight, but it feels like it's become part of me somehow. I just wish I could feel more at ease with myself, like I’m allowed to take up space without all that baggage.

automatic_thoughts: I'm carrying around doubts and worries
I feel trapped in my own head
I wish I could break free and see things differently
It feels like it's become part of me
I wish I could feel more at ease with myself
I'm not allowed to take up space
behaviors: want to shake off that weight

entailment: Fear of being a burden to others (finding)

llm_response.response: It sounds like those doubts and worries are creating a significant emotional burden for you. When you mention feeling trapped and wanting to break free, I wonder if there have been moments recently where you felt lighter or more at ease, even briefly?

patient.query: Yeah, I mean, there have been a couple of times, like when I was out with my friends last weekend. We were just joking around, and I felt that little spark of joy where I could be myself. It was nice, but then a bit later, I started overthinking again, and that weight came back. So, even those moments of lightness feel kind of fleeting, you know? It’s frustrating because I want to hold onto that feeling longer.

automatic_thoughts: I started overthinking again
that weight came back
those moments of lightness feel fleeting

llm_response.response: It sounds like those moments of joy you experienced with friends were brief, which can definitely feel frustrating when you're hoping to hold onto positive feelings. It makes sense that you'd feel that way after the overthinking started again.
